title: leptorkis pectinata
intro: Leptorkis pectinata, also known as the 'Indian Plantain', is a type of flowering plant in the Arum family native to Asia. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Leptorkis pectinata has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, boils, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits such as wound healing, antiseptic properties, and immune system support. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal remedies like powder, paste, or decoction for topical application and oral consumption, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
